note.body: Done
Good catch. Thanks!
Yes! We need it to build and run the `src/server/automerge/migration.ts` file which happens in the apiv1 dockerfile

No problem!

I think stylistically it's because they are overloaded functions, so it creates a feeling of them being grouped. The ones at top are just the overload signatures (new word I just learned).

Ohh yeah good call. I left the block there because I was using off and on so many times and I kept forgetting how to access/decode the message.

Let's chat. I'm curious which part of the socket server setup you're talking about specifically. This portion of the code was written before automerge v3 was released and I haven't cross checked it for currency. There may be some improvements we can make

I only did the date properties that are affected by the Mission automerge document. I considered going all the way. I could pull it out into a separate MR to convert all dates to `getTime` if you think that's best? It would cut down on the migration files at the end, lol.
Excellent excellent. I'm feeling so great about this. This time through I understood so much more having had our recent discussions. All comments low severity.
pretty sure this is dev dep.
Just want to double check that these can't be dev deps.
Can we add some spacing between these functions? I know, nit-picky! also in `mission.ts`

can we prefix these filenames with `crud` like we do `thunk`? I've always found that helpful. I think i'd also like each of these middle-business rule function so have `crud` prefixes like the thunks do so we can recognize them when they're called inline throughout the components.

I love these files btw. Great organization!

Should we put these in as debug level messages when we get the logging stuff ported from CODA but for now I suggest we nuke this block if this was just for dev and we never need to monitor this.

I'm a little fuzzy on why we need to setup the socket server like this for automerge to use and am having trouble seeing where that integration is within this code. Maybe you could show me next time we're chatting?

`.getTime()` ?

Actually I see that many here haven't been converted to this. Did you only touch the ones that touch Mission?

Closed via https://eegitlab.fit.nasa.gov/emss/aegis/-/merge_requests/935

Duplicate issue https://eegitlab.fit.nasa.gov/emss/aegis/-/issues/873

push_data.commit_title: fix reference and typo for jest

note.body: Apologies for missing that. I was looking for the schema check in the pipeline rather than digging into the migration itself.
Ohh that slice wasn't me. I think you did that when you created that component. lol

I went ahead and added a new `useMissionDocSelector` wrapper. Even though it's a tiny 2 line wrapper it did remove quite a bit of lines all around the files. Line diffs for that commit ended up being (+132/-530)
Thanks, it was fixed. I removed the comment

I don't recall, looks like this line was added a year go. I don't have any insight if this was added because of the way automerge fields for lander location were handled at that time, or if this was fixing an existing error if an invalid or empty point was being passed into `thunkGetElevation` (which calls this function `getElevationSinglePoint`). It may have nothing to do with lander location. It is called in many places.
Did a solution search for `const docHandle` and renamed it all to `const missionDocHandle`

Oh I hadn't considered the use case where there might be more than one doc per what is currently "slice". In that case the useDocSelector makes the most sense but even thinking about that, wow what a mess if we need to grab all stations to search for something, etc.
`var(--${type.slice(0, -1)})`  oooo fancy

I like it. I could do this just for mission since it's singular, but I don't know how this would grow when we expand this beyond mission. We haven't really discussed what the granularity of the documents will be when we get to POI, Station, EVA. If we have a document per Station or per EVA then we will still end up with a `useAppSelector` in the component to grab the automerge URL for that station.

The migration file does test the resulting schema. The ci job is running `npm run test:automerge:migration` which generates the schema, builds and runs the migration file. The migration file itself has a bunch of checks. At the end of the script it will run a schema check and push an update to the backup db. I tried to outline that in script comments but I will add more clarity to the top of the job description.
Thanks - added an `opacity` styling. Much better!

note.body: Looking amazing. It's slow going trying to understand everything. Here are a few comments, all for discussion.
Just a reminder in case this really needs to be fixed.
This might be from a previous rev when lag lng could be invalid?
Suggest `missionDocHandle` in anticipation of there being a bunch of different docHandles soon.
Suggest adding some comments explaining that this is only testing that the migrations run and can be run repeatedly. It doesn't check the resulting schema or anything like that.

Suggest making this example have an example of writing a migration function that's repeatable. This example probably is because it's so simple but I'd like to see one that has a "if description already not a property then don't try to remove it" to make the point that migrations will be run multiple times on the same doc.
Is this enough to protect the automerge doc from corruption? Should we have AEGIS disconnect the client from all automerge documents the second we detect the version number isn't the latest?
